steps.title: Identify the form
steps.content: $E(t)=5(1.8)^{t}$ is an exponential function of the form $a\cdot b^{t}$, where $a$ is the initial value and $b$ is the growth factor. Here $a=5$ and $b=1.8$.

steps.title: Evaluate at $t=0$
steps.content: Since $t$ counts years since the restaurant opened, plug in $t=0$: $E(0)=5(1.8)^{0}=5\cdot 1=5$. So $5$ is the number of employees when the restaurant opened.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A) The estimated number of employees when the restaurant opened. This equals $E(0)=5$, which is exactly the role the constant $5$ plays.
  • B) The increase in the estimated number of employees each year. The yearly multiplier is $1.8$, not a constant additive increase, so $5$ does not describe an annual increase.
  • Choice C — The number of years the restaurant has been open The number of years the restaurant has been open. That is the variable $t$, not the constant $5$.
  • D) The percent increase in the estimated number of employees each year. That comes from $b-1=0.8$, i.e., $80\%$, not from the $5$. That is the correct answer.
